pattern: The largest achievable multiple of three from a multiset of digits is built by sorting every digit in descending order and then dropping the fewest, smallest-valued digits needed to fix the digit sum's remainder modulo three.
hints: A number is a multiple of three exactly when the sum of its digits is a multiple of three, so first look at what the total digit sum's remainder mod 3 tells you about whether (and what) you must exclude.

If the remainder is 1, removing a single digit that is itself congruent to 1 (mod 3) fixes it in one step; if no such digit exists, removing two digits that are each congruent to 2 (mod 3) fixes it instead — and symmetrically, a remainder of 2 is fixed by removing one digit congruent to 2, or else two digits congruent to 1.

Among the candidates that share the required remainder, always drop the smallest-valued one(s) so the digits you keep, arranged in descending order, form the largest possible number; if every digit ends up removed the answer is empty, and if the largest remaining digit is 0 the whole answer collapses to a single "0".

edge_inputs.description: typical case, sum already a multiple of three so every digit is kept
single digit that cannot form any multiple of three by itself — impossible, answer is empty
all zeros — must collapse to a single "0", not "000"
removing exactly one digit (the smallest with the matching remainder) fixes the sum
minimum length, single digit already a multiple of three
many identical digits (10 ones): one must be dropped to fix the remainder, leaving the rest — the true 10000-length ceiling is covered by the large-scale stress generator below
the single surviving digit after removal is 0 and 0 — result must be "0" not "00"
no digit alone matches the needed remainder — both same-remainder digits must be dropped, leaving nothing
every digit already individually divisible by three — no removal needed despite each being nonzero

stress.description: random digit multisets of varying length up to 300
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    length = rng.randint(1, size)
    digits = [rng.randint(0, 9) for _ in range(length)]
    return [digits]

stress.description: large-scale random input at the constraint ceiling (10000 digits) to exercise performance and the removal logic at scale
